100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 26, 1926, a significant general strike in Britain continued to disrupt industry and daily life as workers demanded better wages and conditions amid economic hardship. In the United States, President Calvin Coolidge addressed the nation, urging cooperation among business leaders to stabilize the economy. Meanwhile, political tensions escalated in Germany as the Weimar government faced mounting opposition. Across the globe, the League of Nations convened to discuss rising international conflicts, highlighting ongoing challenges in maintaining peace.
